diagrams.title: BFS vs DFS Traversal Order
diagrams.description: Shows the order in which vertices are visited in BFS and DFS starting from vertex A
elements: DFS: A → first neighbor → deep exploration → backtrack → next neighbor
Shows queue/stack contents at each step
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental difference in exploration strategies between BFS and DFS

diagrams.title: DFS Tree and Back Edges
diagrams.description: A directed graph showing DFS tree edges, forward edges, cross edges, and back edges
elements: Tree edges: solid lines in DFS tree
Back edges: dashed lines pointing to ancestors
Forward edges: dashed lines pointing to descendants
Cross edges: dashed lines to vertices in other subtrees
diagrams.purpose: Learn to identify different types of edges in DFS and understand cycle detection

diagrams.title: Connected Components
diagrams.description: A disconnected graph with three connected components
elements: Shows how each component is isolated from others
diagrams.purpose: Visualize what connected components are and how to identify them
